---
title: "Combleaf Mermaidweed (Proserpinaca pectinata)"
slug: "proserpinaca-pectinata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622988254/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Combleaf Mermaidweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Saxifragales"
tax_family: "Haloragaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Saxifragales", "Haloragaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622988254/medium.jpg" alt="Combleaf Mermaidweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jmull28">
jmull28</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342138229">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Saxifragales
-   **Family:** Haloragaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167455)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in still or slow-moving freshwater.

## Natural history notes

An aquatic plant characterized by its finely divided, comb-like submerged leaves and more lance-shaped emergent leaves. In Florida, it is found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats such as ponds, marshes, and ditches. It grows during the warmer months and provides habitat and cover for small aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167455-Proserpinaca-pectinata)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/1002)
